title: A row with weights summing to one preserves constant inputs

source.notes: Generated from checked Lean ground truth; source repository withheld during public release.

informal.statement: If the two row weights sum to one, applying the row to a constant two-vector returns the same constant value.
informal.latex: a+b=1 implies a u + b u = u.
assumptions: a, b, and u are real numbers
the row is applied to the constant vector (u,u)

declarations.name: row2Apply
declarations.statement: def row2Apply (a b x y : Real) : Real := a * x + b * y

declarations.name: row2Apply_const
declarations.statement: theorem row2Apply_const (a b u : Real) (hsum : a + b = 1) : row2Apply a b u u = u

tasks.input: Translate constant-vector preservation for a two-entry row whose weights sum to one into Lean 4.
tasks.target: theorem row2Apply_const (a b u : Real) (hsum : a + b = 1) : row2Apply a b u u = u

tasks.input: Complete the Lean proof by unfolding row2Apply and rewriting with the row-sum assumption.
tasks.target: Unfold row2Apply, factor a*u+b*u as (a+b)*u, rewrite hsum, and simplify.
